name: Coming of Age
description: A young protagonist matures through formative experiences, achieving psychological or moral growth.

nodes.label: Innocence
nodes.definition: The protagonist exists in a state of naivety, dependence, or incomplete understanding of the world. Their identity is defined by their sheltered context.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the character's youth, naivety, or limited worldview
exit_conditions: An experience forces the character to confront something beyond their current understanding
typical_variants: Child in a protective household
Teenager with an untested worldview
Young person on the cusp of a transition (new school, first job, move)
failure_modes: Innocence is so extreme the character seems incompetent rather than young
The sheltered world is never established, so loss of innocence has no impact

nodes.label: First Exposure
nodes.definition: The protagonist encounters something that doesn't fit their existing worldview — injustice, mortality, adult complexity, or their own desire. The comfortable frame cracks.
entry_conditions: An experience intrudes that the character cannot process with existing tools
exit_conditions: The character is unsettled; the old worldview is no longer sufficient
typical_variants: Witnessing injustice or hypocrisy
First encounter with death or loss
Discovering a parent's or authority figure's flaw
First romantic or sexual experience
failure_modes: Exposure is too gentle to disturb anything
Exposure is so traumatic it overwhelms the story's tone

nodes.label: Testing Ground
nodes.definition: The protagonist enters a social arena where their identity, beliefs, and relationships are tested — a new school, peer group, community, or challenge.
entry_conditions: The character enters a new or intensified social context
exit_conditions: The character has been tested enough that their identity is in flux
typical_variants: New school or social environment
First independent journey
Peer group with different values
A challenge that requires new capabilities
failure_modes: Testing ground is too easy — no real pressure on identity
Tests are purely external with no impact on the character's self-understanding
signals_in_text: Trying on new identities or behaviors
Success and failure in new contexts

nodes.label: Mentor or Role Model
nodes.definition: The protagonist encounters a figure who represents a possible future self — an adult, older peer, or teacher who embodies values the character is beginning to form.
entry_conditions: The character is in the testing ground
They need guidance or a model
exit_conditions: The character has internalized something from the mentor, even if they later diverge
typical_variants: Inspiring teacher
Older friend or sibling figure
Flawed adult who teaches by negative example
A book, film, or idea that reshapes thinking
failure_modes: Mentor provides answers rather than questions
signals_in_text: Advice that the character doesn't fully understand yet

nodes.label: Crisis of Identity
nodes.definition: The protagonist faces a situation that forces a definitive choice about who they are — a moral test, a loyalty conflict, or a moment where childhood beliefs and adult reality collide irreconcilably.
entry_conditions: Testing and mentorship have developed the character but not resolved their identity
A situation demands a defining choice
exit_conditions: The character makes a choice that commits them to a version of themselves
typical_variants: Choosing between peer group and personal values
Standing up to authority at personal cost
Accepting a painful truth about someone they admire
First act of genuine moral courage or serious moral failure
failure_modes: Crisis is too small to be defining
The right choice is obvious — no real tension
Character is passive and the crisis resolves around them
signals_in_text: Doing something for the first time that defines who they'll become

nodes.label: Loss of Innocence
nodes.definition: The protagonist permanently loses their naive worldview. This is not just learning — it is the irreversible end of a simpler way of seeing. Something is gained but something is also gone forever.
entry_conditions: The character has seen or done something that cannot be unseen or undone
exit_conditions: The character carries this knowledge forward into their new identity
typical_variants: Realizing the world is unjust and will not fix itself
Understanding that people they trusted are flawed or wrong
Accepting responsibility for a consequence of their own choice
Understanding mortality or irreversible loss
failure_modes: Loss is sentimental rather than genuinely costly
The character loses innocence but gains nothing — it's just bleak
Loss is stated but the character's behavior doesn't change
signals_in_text: Tears or silence rather than action
Looking at familiar things with new eyes

nodes.label: New Maturity
nodes.definition: The protagonist has crossed into a more adult understanding of the world. They are not yet fully formed, but they are no longer the person they were. The story closes on a character poised between what they were and what they will become.
entry_conditions: The character has made a defining choice
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established — the character's identity has shifted
typical_variants: Quiet acceptance and forward motion
Bittersweet farewell to childhood
A first adult relationship or responsibility
Returning to a familiar setting and seeing it differently
failure_modes: Maturity is too complete — the character is suddenly a wise adult
No visible difference from the opening — the experiences left no mark
Ending is purely sad with no sense of growth
signals_in_text: Changed relationships with parents or authority
A small act that shows new understanding

edges.label: World Cracks Open
edges.preconditions: Innocence is established; an experience intrudes that the character cannot process
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character's comfortable worldview is threatened
edges.effects_on_character: First discomfort; questions arise that can't be answered by the old framework
edges.common_alternatives: Exposure is gradual; exposure is a single shocking event
edges.anti_patterns: Exposure is so mild it doesn't disturb anything

edges.label: Enters the Arena
edges.preconditions: First exposure has destabilized the character; a new context demands engagement
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character must navigate without the safety net of innocence
edges.effects_on_character: Identity becomes fluid — the character tries on new selves
edges.common_alternatives: Enters voluntarily out of curiosity; is thrust into the testing ground by circumstance
edges.anti_patterns: Character enters but is never tested — the arena is just a backdrop

edges.label: Finds a Model
edges.preconditions: The character is navigating the testing ground and needs guidance
edges.effects_on_stakes: A possible path forward becomes visible through the mentor's example
edges.effects_on_character: The character begins to form values consciously rather than inheriting them
edges.common_alternatives: Mentor is a positive model; mentor is a cautionary example
edges.anti_patterns: Mentor provides all answers and removes the need for the character to struggle

edges.label: Identity Put to the Test
edges.preconditions: The character has developing values; a situation demands they act on them
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character's emerging identity is at risk — they may fail the test
edges.effects_on_character: Everything learned so far converges into a single defining moment
edges.common_alternatives: Crisis directly challenges the mentor's teachings; crisis forces a choice between two valued things
edges.anti_patterns: Crisis has an obvious right answer with no real cost

edges.label: Pays the Price of Knowing
edges.preconditions: The crisis has been faced; the character has acted
edges.effects_on_stakes: Something is permanently lost — a relationship, a belief, a version of the self
edges.effects_on_character: The character can never return to the innocent state; knowledge is irreversible
edges.common_alternatives: Loss is of a relationship; loss is of a belief; loss is of a period of life
edges.anti_patterns: Loss is purely sentimental with no real impact on the character's future

edges.label: Grows Up
edges.preconditions: The loss is internalized; the character has been changed by the experience
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes shift from external events to the character's internal state
edges.effects_on_character: A new, more complex identity stabilizes — still forming but fundamentally different
edges.common_alternatives: Maturity is quiet and internal; maturity is demonstrated through a visible act
edges.anti_patterns: Character is instantly wise and complete rather than on the threshold of ongoing growth
